skills.name: code-review
skills.description: Performs code reviews following Sentry engineering practices. Covers runtime errors, N+1 queries, security vulnerabilities, test coverage, and long-term impact assessment.

skills.name: code-review-pro
skills.description: Deep parallel code analysis using distributed agents — each file reviewed independently, findings merged. Security, performance, code quality, best practices, bugs with severity ratings.

skills.name: find-bugs
skills.description: Reviews uncommitted changes on current branch for bugs, security vulnerabilities, and code quality issues. Maps attack surface and runs full security checklist.

skills.name: security-review
skills.description: Comprehensive security checklist: secrets management, input validation, SQL injection, auth/authz, XSS, CSRF, rate limiting, dependency security.

skills.name: code-simplifier
skills.description: Simplifies and refines code for clarity, consistency, and maintainability. Preserves functionality, applies project-specific best practices.

skills.name: feature-dev
skills.description: Guided feature development with 8 phases: Discovery, Codebase Exploration, Clarifying Questions, Architecture Design, Implementation, Testing, Quality Review, Summary.

skills.name: tdd
skills.description: Test-driven development using red-green-refactor loop. Emphasizes integration-style tests through public interfaces using vertical slices.

skills.name: git-commit
skills.description: Executes git commit with conventional commit message analysis, intelligent staging, and message generation.

skills.name: command-creator
skills.description: Creates Claude Code slash commands (.claude/commands/) with proper structure and best practices.

skills.name: skill-scanner
skills.description: Scans agent skills for security issues: prompt injection, malicious code, excessive permissions, secret exposure.

skills.name: agents-run
skills.description: Spawns parallel general-purpose subagents on complex requests, coordinates, assigns work packages, synthesizes results.

skills.name: subagent-creator
skills.description: Creates specialized Claude Code sub-agents with custom system prompts and tool configurations.

skills.name: skill-creator
skills.description: Creates, modifies, and evaluates skills with quantitative benchmarking and browser-based eval viewer.

skills.name: find-skills
skills.description: Discovers and installs skills from the open ecosystem via agentskill.sh. Verifies install counts and source reputation.

skills.name: learn
skills.description: Discovers, installs, and manages AI agent skills from agentskill.sh. Full CLI, security scanning, context-aware recommendations.

skills.name: init-rules
skills.description: Initializes CLAUDE.md coding standards by auto-detecting tech stack and assembling rules from template library.

skills.name: web-coder
skills.description: Expert 10x web development across 15 domains: HTML, CSS, JS, Web APIs, HTTP, security, performance, accessibility.

skills.name: make-interfaces-feel-better
skills.description: Design engineering principles for UI polish: concentric border radius, optical alignment, shadows, interruptible animations, stagger effects.

skills.name: emil-design-eng
skills.description: Design engineering philosophy for animation decisions: spring animations, CSS transforms, clip-path patterns, gesture interactions.

skills.name: frontend-slides
skills.description: Creates zero-dependency, animation-rich HTML presentations with PPTX conversion and keyboard/touch navigation.

skills.name: autobrowse
skills.description: Self-improving browser automation via iterative evaluate-read-improve strategy. Trains skills for specific sites.

skills.name: agents-md
skills.description: Creates and maintains AGENTS.md agent-facing documentation. Target: under 60 lines, never 100.

skills.name: create-AGENTS.md
skills.description: Creates minimal AGENTS.md file with project sections alongside companion CLAUDE.md.

skills.name: create-readme
skills.description: Creates comprehensive README.md files inspired by Azure-Samples open source examples.

skills.name: obsidian-markdown
skills.description: Creates Obsidian Flavored Markdown with wikilinks, embeds, callouts, properties, tags, LaTeX math, Mermaid diagrams.

skills.name: pdf
skills.description: PDF operations: reading (pypdf, pdfplumber), creating (reportlab), merging, splitting, OCR, watermarking, password protection.

skills.name: docx
skills.description: Word document creation, reading, editing using docx npm library and Python scripts. Tables, images, tracked changes.

skills.name: dbhub
skills.description: Database exploration via DBHub MCP server with explore-first workflow (schema → tables → columns → indexes → query).

skills.name: caveman
skills.description: Ultra-compressed communication mode, reducing token usage ~75%. Drops articles, filler, pleasantries.

skills.name: caveman-compress
skills.description: Compresses memory files (CLAUDE.md, todos) into caveman format to save tokens.

skills.name: plain-english
skills.description: Explains complex topics in simple terms at 3 levels: Simple (default), Age 10, Age 15.

skills.name: grill-me
skills.description: Interviews user relentlessly about every aspect of a plan until reaching shared understanding.

skills.name: modern-python
skills.description: Configures Python projects with modern tooling: uv, ruff, ty. Anti-patterns to avoid and migration guides.

skills.name: gh-cli
skills.description: Comprehensive GitHub CLI reference covering repos, issues, PRs, Actions, projects, releases, gists, codespaces.

skills.name: mcp2cli
skills.description: Turns MCP servers, OpenAPI specs, GraphQL endpoints into CLIs at runtime without codegen.

skills.name: mcp-context7
skills.description: Fetches current library/framework documentation via Context7 MCP server.

skills.name: mcp-tavily-mcp
skills.description: Web search, content extraction, and research via Tavily MCP.

skills.name: mcp-notion
skills.description: Full Notion integration: search, fetch, create/update pages and databases, comments.

skills.name: debug-mode
skills.description: Hypothesis-driven interactive debugging with phases: Understand → Hypotheses → Instrument → Analyze → Fix → Verify.

skills.name: weather
skills.description: Weather forecasts via Open-Meteo API. Hourly (12h) and daily (15d) forecasts.

skills.name: daily-ai
skills.description: Daily AI industry digest from 6 podcasts, 25 X builders, 2 blogs. GitHub Actions with ETag caching.

skills.name: delayed-command
skills.description: Waits specified duration, then executes bash command. Format: 30s, 5m, 1h, 1h30m.

skills.name: toggle-bypassPermissions
skills.description: Toggles bypassPermissions in project settings.local.json.

skills.name: import-export-claude-global-profile
skills.description: Exports, imports, compares Claude Code global settings with backup folder or GitHub repo.

skills.name: improve-codebase-architecture
skills.description: Explores codebase for architectural improvement, focusing on testability. Creates GitHub issue RFC.

skills.name: codebase-to-course
skills.description: Transforms codebase into interactive HTML course with scroll navigation, visualizations, quizzes.

skills.name: prompt-master
skills.description: Generates optimized prompts for any AI tool (Claude, GPT, o3, Gemini, Midjourney, etc.). Avoids fabricated techniques.

newEmptyProject.purpose: Initialize coding standards based on tech stack
Set up Python project with uv, ruff, ty

existingProject.purpose: Find relevant skills for the task

newEmptyProject.prompt: Initialize my coding standards for a new Python project
newEmptyProject.when: Starting a new project - auto-detects tech stack and creates CLAUDE.md with appropriate rules

newEmptyProject.prompt: Set up a modern Python project structure with uv, ruff, and ty
newEmptyProject.when: After init-rules - configures pyproject.toml, pre-commit, security tooling

newEmptyProject.prompt: Create AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md files for my new project
I need to build a user authentication system with JWT tokens and password hashing
newEmptyProject.example: /feature-dev then describe: Build a REST API for user registration and login with JWT auth
newEmptyProject.when: Ready to implement first feature - guided 8-phase development

newEmptyProject.prompt: Write tests first for the user registration endpoint
newEmptyProject.when: During feature-dev implementation - write tests before code

newEmptyProject.prompt: Simplify the authentication module I just wrote
newEmptyProject.when: After feature implementation - clean up before review

newEmptyProject.prompt: Review my auth module thoroughly for security and performance issues
Run a security checklist on the authentication implementation

newEmptyProject.when: After all reviews pass - conventional commit message

newEmptyProject.prompt: Create a README.md for my new project
newEmptyProject.when: Project ready for sharing - comprehensive documentation

existingProject.prompt: Find skills for building a GraphQL API
existingProject.example: /learn find skills for graphql api development
existingProject.when: Starting new work - discover available skills

existingProject.prompt: Get the latest documentation for FastAPI and Pydantic v2
existingProject.example: resolve-library-id: fastapi, then query-docs: validation patterns
existingProject.when: Before implementation - ensure using current best practices

existingProject.prompt: Add a file upload endpoint to the existing API
existingProject.example: /feature-dev then describe: Add async file upload with S3 storage
existingProject.when: Ready to implement - systematic feature development

existingProject.prompt: TDD approach for the file upload feature
existingProject.example: /tdd — test the upload endpoint with various file types

existingProject.prompt: Debug why the upload fails for files larger than 5MB
existingProject.when: Tests fail or bugs appear - structured hypothesis debugging
After fixing bugs - clean up before review

existingProject.prompt: Check for bugs in my uncommitted changes
existingProject.when: Before commit - security and bug scan

existingProject.prompt: Security checklist for file upload feature
existingProject.when: Before merge - especially important for file handling
